source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Security Configurations are Reviewed Regularly

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Security Configurations are Reviewed Regularly
input_row.description: Regularly updating and reviewing the security configuration of your Amazon ElastiCache clusters helps ensure that your clusters are protected against potential vulnerabilities and aligned with your security requirements.
input_row.rationale: This ensures that the clusters are being regularly updated and protected from any potential vulnerabilities as well as meeting the security requirements. Impact: Updating the system and being updated with security configurations keeps everything secure and prevents it from an attack.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console • Sign in to the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the ElastiCache Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/elasticache/. 3. Select the ElastiCache Cluster • Choose the ElastiCache cluster you want to update and review the security configuration. Click on the cluster name to access its details page. 4. Review IAM Policies • Navigate to the Configuration tab on the cluster details page. • Click on the IAM Access tab. • Review the IAM policies associated with the ElastiCache cluster and its resources. • Ensure that the IAM policies provide the least privileged access, granting only the necessary permissions to users and roles.  • Update the IAM policies as required based on changes in access requirements or security best practices. 5. Review Security Groups • Navigate to the Configuration tab on the cluster details page. • Click on the Security Groups tab. • Review the security groups associated with the ElastiCache cluster. • Ensure that the inbound and outbound rules of the security groups are configured correctly and restrict access to necessary ports and IP ranges. • Update the security group rules as needed to align with your security requirements. 6. Review Encryption Settings • Navigate to the Configuration tab on the cluster details page. • Click on the Encryption at Rest tab. • Verify the encryption settings for the ElastiCache cluster. • Ensure that encryption at rest is enabled and using the appropriate encryption type (default AWS-managed key or customer-managed key). • Update the encryption settings if necessary to comply with your security policies. 7. Review Network Security • Navigate to the Configuration tab on the cluster details page. • Click on the Network & Security tab. • Review the VPC, subnets, security groups, and network ACLs associated with the ElastiCache cluster. • Ensure that the VPC and subnet configurations align with your security requirements. • Update the network security settings as needed to maintain a secure network architecture. 8. Review Access Control • Navigate to the Configuration tab on the cluster details page. • Click on the Security tab. • Review the authentication and access control settings for the ElastiCache cluster. • Ensure that the authentication method (no password, transit encryption, or encryption in transit) meets your security standards. • Update the access control settings as required to align with your security policies. 9. Regularly Monitor Security Bulletins • Stay updated with AWS security bulletins, advisories, and best practices.  • Regularly review security-related announcements from AWS. • Take necessary actions based on security recommendations, such as applying patches or configuration changes.
input_row.impact: Updating the system and being updated with security configurations keeps everything secure and prevents it from an attack.
input_row.justification: While many individual ElastiCache security configurations (encryption, security groups, IAM roles) can be programmatically checked using AWS APIs, the control explicitly requires a comprehensive, contextual review—including alignment with organizational policies, interpretation of least privilege, and monitoring of AWS security bulletins. These aspects inherently require human judgment and cannot be fully automated. Therefore, a manual approach is necessary to ensure compliance with the intent and depth of this control.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. **Sign in to the AWS Management Console:** Go to https://console.aws.amazon.com/ and log in with appropriate credentials. 2. **Navigate to ElastiCache:** In the AWS Console, use the search bar to find and open the ElastiCache service. 3. **Select Each ElastiCache Cluster:** For each cluster, click on its name to access the details page. 4. **Review Security Configurations:** - **Encryption:** Confirm that both encryption at-rest and in-transit are enabled. - **Security Groups:** Review attached security groups to ensure only necessary ports are open and access is restricted to trusted sources. - **IAM Roles/Policies:** Examine associated IAM roles and policies for least privilege and adherence to organizational standards. - **Parameter Groups:** Check for secure parameter settings (e.g., AUTH tokens, TLS versions). - **Maintenance & Updates:** Verify that the cluster is set to receive regular maintenance and patching. 5. **Cross-Reference Organizational Policies:** Compare current configurations with your organization’s security policies and CIS recommendations to ensure alignment. 6. **Monitor AWS Security Bulletins:** Review recent AWS security bulletins and advisories for ElastiCache and confirm that any relevant mitigations or updates have been applied. 7. **Document Findings:** Record the review date, findings, and any remediation actions taken or required.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
Given the detailed nature of the control, which requires not only review of specific configurations but also alignment with organizational policies, interpreting least privilege, and staying updated with security bulletins, it entails human judgment that cannot be fully automated. While many individual elements such as IAM policies, security groups, and encryption settings can be queried via AWS APIs or verified through CLI commands, the overall security integrity and contextual review required necessitate a manual approach.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Sign in to the AWS Management Console:**
   - Go to https://console.aws.amazon.com/ and log in with your appropriate credentials.
   
2. **Navigate to ElastiCache:**
   - In the AWS Console, use the search bar to find and open the ElastiCache service.

3. **Select Each ElastiCache Cluster:**
   - For each ElastiCache cluster, click on its name to access the details page.

4. **Review Security Configurations:**
   - **Encryption:**
     - Verify that both encryption at rest and in transit are enabled.
   - **Security Groups:**
     - Review the attached security groups to ensure only necessary ports are open and access is restricted to trusted sources.
   - **IAM Roles/Policies:**
     - Examine associated IAM roles and policies for least privilege and adherence to organizational standards.
   - **Parameter Groups:**
     - Check for secure parameter settings (e.g., AUTH tokens, TLS versions).
   - **Maintenance & Updates:**
     - Verify that the cluster is set to receive regular maintenance and patching.

5. **Cross-Reference Organizational Policies:**
   - Compare current configurations with your organization’s security policies and CIS recommendations to ensure alignment.

6. **Monitor AWS Security Bulletins:**
   - Review recent AWS security bulletins and advisories specific to ElastiCache and confirm that any relevant mitigations or updates have been applied.

7. **Document Findings:**
   - Record the review date, findings, and any remediation actions taken or required.
